title: dryopteris calcarea
intro: Dryopteris calcarea, also known as the calcareous wood fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the Dryopteris genus. It is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia, and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The herbal preparations of Dryopteris calcarea include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to promote overall health and well-being.
